message:
feat(db,search,tools,tui,guard,cli): multi-crate reliability, search UX, and reservation improvements

DB query optimization (queries.rs):
- Replace full-table project scans with parameterized WHERE queries
  using slug/human_key indexes, eliminating O(n) Rust-side filtering
- Use Path::is_absolute() instead of starts_with('/') for cross-platform
  correctness in project key resolution
- Refactor nested match arms to idiomatic map_or/map_or_else

Search improvements (search_service, search_planner, tui/search, spec):
- Fix planner edge case: empty extract_like_terms with non-empty query
  text now correctly falls through to PlanMethod::Empty instead of
  generating broken LIKE clauses
- Add SearchDegradedDiagnostics and derive_tui_degraded_diagnostics()
  to surface fallback mode, budget governor pressure, and timeout
  stages in the TUI search cockpit
- Add ZeroResultGuidance rendering for empty search results
- Expand unit tests for guidance generation and plan serialization
- Update search v3 query contract spec documentation

Messaging (messaging.rs):
- Relax recipient validation: allow sending when any of to/cc/bcc is
  non-empty (previously required non-empty 'to')
- Add post-inline size re-validation to catch body expansion from
  image inlining exceeding max_message_body_bytes

File reservations & guard (reservations.rs, guard/lib.rs, cli/lib.rs):
- Add relativize_path() to normalize absolute reservation paths
  relative to project root with directory traversal detection
- Refine guard conflict matching: symmetric fnmatch plus explicit
  directory-prefix matching replacing fragile startswith approach
- Propagate resolved SQLite DB path through install_guard so the
  generated pre-commit hook finds the database without env vars

CLI improvements (cli/lib.rs, main.rs, robot.rs):
- Graceful port cleanup: kill_port_holder now tries SIGTERM first,
  waits 1s, then escalates to SIGKILL only if port still held
- Wire new guard installation parameters through handle_guard

TUI analytics (tui_screens/analytics.rs):
- Add persisted tool metrics fallback: when live quick_insight_feed
  returns no cards, build insight feed from historical data generating
  AnomalyAlert cards for high error rates and latency spikes

Minor fixes across crates:
- core: config expansion, diagnostics refinements, flake triage
  improvements, memory fix
- db: pool and tracking minor adjustments
- server: cleanup refactor, startup checks, tool metrics, tui_app
- share: bundle, scrub, snapshot improvements
- tools: products, resources, search refinements

Also syncs beads issue tracker metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-2tnl.6.7","title":"T6.7: Add filter/date ergonomics (aliases + timezone normalization + boundary semantics)","description":"## Task
Harden filter ergonomics so users can reliably express sender/project/date constraints with minimal friction and no ambiguity.

## Scope
- Field aliases (`sender`, `from_agent`, etc.) mapped deterministically to canonical fields.
- Date/time parsing normalized to UTC with explicit inclusive/exclusive boundary behavior.
- Consistent filter UX semantics across MCP and TUI surfaces.

## Deliverable
Predictable filter behavior for real-world user inputs, including cross-timezone and mixed-format date ranges.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Field alias mapping for sender/project/date filters is deterministic and shared by MCP and TUI.
2. Date/time parsing and boundary semantics are normalized to UTC with explicit inclusive/exclusive behavior.
3. Unit and integration tests cover timezone edge cases, ambiguous input handling, and stable serialization of normalized filters.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","notes":"Implemented deterministic sender/project/date alias mapping and UTC-normalized boundary semantics across search tool surfaces.
